pattern: Every internal node multiplies two subtree maxima, so greedily removing the smallest leaf by pairing it with its smaller neighbor (a monotonic-stack sweep) minimizes the total product cost.
hints: Each non-leaf value is the product of the max leaf on its left and the max leaf on its right, so a small leaf squeezed between two larger ones will be multiplied once by the cheaper neighbor and then disappear.
Think about which leaf to merge away first: the smallest one, paired with the smaller of its two neighbors, to keep its contribution minimal.
Sweep with a decreasing monotonic stack; when the next value is larger than the top, pop the top and add top*min(next, new-top), since that popped leaf can no longer be anyone's max.

edge_inputs.description: small leaf between two larger leaves
minimum length, only one tree shape
strictly increasing favors merging from the small end
strictly decreasing is the mirror case
valley shape with a dip in the middle

stress.description: random small arrays within the tight value bound
stress.note: length kept modest so the cubic DP oracle stays fast
